#review #IELTS #B2 #EnglishFreeCourse

posts.title: Noma'lum mavzuда gapirish
posts.text:
🗣 Noma'lum mavzuда gapirish

Imtihonda bilmagan mavzu chiqsa — to'xtab qolmaslik muhim. "Vaqt yutuvchi" iboralar yordam beradi:
• That's an interesting question ... — Qiziq savol ...
• Let me think for a moment ... — Bir o'ylab ko'ray ...
• Well, it's hard to say, but ... — Aytish qiyin, lekin ...

Bu iboralar jimlikni to'ldiradi va tabiiy ko'rinadi.

📚 Manba: IELTS.org — Speaking fluency

#speaking #IELTS #B2 #EnglishFreeCourse

posts.title: Paraphrasing — so'z esga tushmasa
posts.text:
💬 Paraphrasing — so'z esga tushmasa

So'z yodingizdan chiqsa, BOSHQACHA tushuntiring:
• "It's a kind of ..." — Bu bir turdagi ...
• "It's the thing you use to ..." — Bu ... uchun ishlatadigan narsa
• "It's similar to ..." — Bu ... ga o'xshaydi

Misol: "thermometer" esingizда yo'q → "the thing you use to measure temperature".

#speaking #IELTS #B2 #EnglishFreeCourse

posts.title: Linking words (Speaking uchun)
posts.text:
🎯 Linking words (Speaking uchun)

Tabiiy bog'lovchilar ravonlik beradi:
• and then, after that — keyin
• because, so — chunki, shuning uchun
• but, although — lekin, garchi
• for example, like — masalan
⚠️ Yozma bog'lovchilarni (moreover, furthermore) Speaking'da kam ishlating — sun'iy eshitiladi.

#speaking #IELTS #B2 #EnglishFreeCourse

posts.title: Homework + Useful word
posts.text:
📝 Homework + Useful word

Tasodifiy mavzu tanlang (masalan "robots") va 1 daqiqa TO'XTAMASDAN gapiring. To'xtab qolsangiz, "vaqt yutuvchi" iborani ishlating.

💡 Useful word: fluent /ˈfluː.ənt/ — ravon (gapni erkin va to'xtamasdan ayta oladigan)

#speaking #IELTS #B2 #EnglishFreeCourse

posts.title: Eng ko'p uchraydigan xatolar
posts.text:
✍️ Eng ko'p uchraydigan xatolar

IELTS Writing'da B2 o'quvchilar ko'p qiladigan xatolar:
• Artikl: "I have car" → "I have A car"
• Birlik/ko'plik: "many informations" → "much information"
• Zamon: gap o'rtasida zamonни almashtirib yuborish
• So'z tartibi: "I like very much it" → "I like it very much"

📚 Manba: Cambridge English — Common errors at B2

#writing #IELTS #B2 #EnglishFreeCourse

posts.title: Sanab bo'lmaydigan otlar (uncountable)
posts.text:
💬 Sanab bo'lmaydigan otlar (uncountable)

Bular ALWAYS birlik, "a/many" olmaydi:
• information, advice, news, knowledge, research, furniture, money, traffic
❌ "an advice / many advices"
✅ "some advice / a piece of advice"

#writing #IELTS #B2 #EnglishFreeCourse

posts.title: Tahrir (editing) — oxirgi 3 daqiqa
posts.text:
🎯 Tahrir (editing) — oxirgi 3 daqiqa

Yozib bo'lgach, tekshiring:
1. Har gapda fe'l (verb) bormi?
2. Artikllar (a/an/the) joyidami?
3. Birlik/ko'plik to'g'rimi (-s)?
4. So'z sonini sanang (Task 2 ≥ 250, Task 1 ≥ 150).

Tahrir 1 band ko'tarishi mumkin!

#writing #IELTS #B2 #EnglishFreeCourse

posts.title: Homework + Useful word
posts.text:
📝 Homework + Useful word

Xatoni toping va tuzating:
1. "She gave me an useful advice."
2. "There is many traffic today."
(Javob: 1 a useful piece of advice / some useful advice. 2 There is a lot of traffic today.)

🎉 30 kunlik kursni tugatdingiz! Davom eting va IELTS'ни zabt eting!

💡 Useful word: accuracy /ˈæk.jə.rə.si/ — aniqlik, xatosizlik

#writing #IELTS #B2 #EnglishFreeCourse

archived_posts.title: 3 Powerful Verbs
archived_posts.text:
🔤 WORD OF THE DAY

📌 achieve /əˈtʃiːv/ — erishmoq
📌 improve /ɪmˈpruːv/ — yaxshilamoq
📌 overcome /ˌəʊvəˈkʌm/ — yengmoq, bartaraf etmoq

💬 With daily practice, you can overcome any difficulty and achieve your goals.
🇺🇿 Har kunlik mashq bilan har qanday qiyinchilikni yengib, maqsadingizga erishasiz.

archived_posts.title: Octopus has 3 hearts
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

An octopus has three hearts and blue blood. Two hearts pump blood to the gills, and one pumps it to the rest of the body.

🇺🇿 Sakkizoyoqning uchta yuragi va ko'k qoni bor. Ikki yurak qonni jabralarga, biri esa butun tanaga haydaydi.

📚 Murakkab so'zlar: gills /ɡɪlz/ — jabralar; pump /pʌmp/ — haydamoq

archived_posts.title: Cleopatra & the Pyramids
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

Cleopatra lived closer in time to the first Moon landing (1969) than to the building of the Great Pyramid. The Pyramid was completed around 2560 BC — about 2,500 years before her birth.

🇺🇿 Kleopatra Buyuk Piramida qurilishidan (mil. av. ~2560) ko'ra, 1969-yilgi Oyga qo'nishga vaqt jihatdan yaqinroq yashagan. Piramida undan ~2500 yil oldin qurilgan.

📚 So'zlar: ancient /ˈeɪnʃənt/ — qadimiy; century /ˈsentʃəri/ — asr

archived_posts.title: The Lion and the Mouse
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Lion and the Mouse

A tiny mouse woke a sleeping lion. The lion caught it, but the mouse begged for mercy, so the lion let it go. Later, hunters trapped the lion in a net. The little mouse heard him roar and chewed the ropes until the lion was free.

💡 Moral: Even the small can help the mighty.
🇺🇿 Kichik sichqon uxlayotgan sherni uyg'otadi. Sher uni kechiradi. Keyin sher to'rga tushganda, sichqon arqonlarni kemirib uni qutqaradi. Xulosa: kichik ham kuchliga yordam bera oladi.

📚 mercy /ˈmɜːsi/ — rahm; net /net/ — to'r; chew /tʃuː/ — kemirmoq

archived_posts.title: Grammar Quiz
archived_posts.text:
📝 GRAMMAR TEST

She ____ to school every day.

A) go
B) goes
C) going
D) gone

💭 Javobni o'ylab toping! 👇
archived_posts.answer:
✅ To'g'ri javob: B) goes

📖 Present Simple'da he/she/it bilan fe'lga -s/-es qo'shiladi: she goes, he reads, it works.

archived_posts.title: Samarkand — 2750 years
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY · Uzbekistan

Samarkand is over 2,750 years old — one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in Central Asia. It was a key city on the Silk Road, and in 1370 the ruler Amir Temur (Tamerlane) made it his capital.

🇺🇿 Samarqand 2750 yildan oshiq tarixga ega — Markaziy Osiyodagi eng qadimiy shaharlardan biri. Buyuk Ipak yo'lining muhim shahri; 1370-yilda Amir Temur uni poytaxt qilgan.

📚 So'zlar: inhabited /ɪnˈhæbɪtɪd/ — aholi yashaydigan; capital /ˈkæpɪtl/ — poytaxt

archived_posts.title: Lightning vs the Sun
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

A bolt of lightning can reach about 30,000°C — that is roughly five times hotter than the surface of the Sun (~5,500°C).

🇺🇿 Chaqmoq harorati ~30,000°C ga yetadi — bu Quyosh sirtidan (~5,500°C) qariyb besh barobar issiqroq.

📚 So'zlar: lightning /ˈlaɪtnɪŋ/ — chaqmoq; surface /ˈsɜːfɪs/ — sirt, yuza

archived_posts.title: IELTS Trend Words
archived_posts.text:
🔤 IELTS VOCABULARY

📌 significant /sɪɡˈnɪfɪkənt/ — sezilarli, muhim
📌 gradual /ˈɡrædʒuəl/ — bosqichma-bosqich
📌 fluctuate /ˈflʌktʃueɪt/ — o'zgarib turmoq

💬 Prices fluctuated in spring but showed a significant rise by the end of the year.
🇺🇿 Bahorda narxlar o'zgarib turdi, lekin yil oxiriga sezilarli o'sdi.

archived_posts.title: The Tortoise and the Hare
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Tortoise and the Hare

A fast hare laughed at a slow tortoise and challenged him to a race. The hare ran ahead, then stopped to nap, sure he would win. The tortoise kept moving slowly without stopping — and crossed the finish line first.

💡 Moral: Slow and steady wins the race.
🇺🇿 Tez quyon sekin toshbaqani masxara qilib, poygaga chaqiradi. Quyon uxlab qoladi; toshbaqa to'xtamay yurib, birinchi keladi. Xulosa: sekin, lekin barqaror harakat g'alaba keltiradi.

📚 hare /heə/ — quyon; nap /næp/ — mizg'imoq; steady /ˈstedi/ — barqaror

archived_posts.title: Bananas are berries
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Botanically, a banana is a berry, but a strawberry is not! A true berry grows from one flower with one ovary — bananas qualify, strawberries do not.

🇺🇿 Botanika nuqtai nazaridan banan — rezavor meva (berry), lekin qulupnay emas! Haqiqiy berry bitta gulning bitta tugunchasidan o'sadi.

📚 So'zlar: berry /ˈberi/ — rezavor meva; flower /ˈflaʊə/ — gul

archived_posts.title: Idiom: Piece of cake
archived_posts.text:
🗣 IDIOM OF THE DAY

🍰 "a piece of cake" — juda oson ish

💬 The test was a piece of cake for her.
🇺🇿 So'zma-so'z "bir bo'lak tort" emas — ma'nosi "juda oson". Suhbatda tabiiy eshitiladi: The exam was a piece of cake.

📚 piece /piːs/ — bo'lak; easy /ˈiːzi/ — oson

archived_posts.title: Shortest war: 38 min
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

The shortest war in recorded history was the Anglo-Zanzibar War (27 August 1896). It lasted only about 38 minutes, from 09:02 to 09:40.

🇺🇿 Tarixdagi eng qisqa urush — Angliya-Zanzibar urushi (1896-yil 27-avgust). U atigi ~38 daqiqa davom etgan (soat 09:02 dan 09:40 gacha).

📚 So'zlar: war /wɔː/ — urush; recorded /rɪˈkɔːdɪd/ — qayd etilgan; last /lɑːst/ — davom etmoq

archived_posts.title: Advanced Words
archived_posts.text:
🔤 ADVANCED VOCABULARY

📌 inevitable /ɪnˈevɪtəbl/ — muqarrar
📌 profound /prəˈfaʊnd/ — chuqur, kuchli
📌 mitigate /ˈmɪtɪɡeɪt/ — yumshatmoq, kamaytirmoq

💬 Technology has a profound impact on society, and change is inevitable.
🇺🇿 Texnologiya jamiyatga chuqur ta'sir qiladi, o'zgarish esa muqarrar.

archived_posts.title: Sunlight takes 8 minutes
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth. So you always see the Sun as it looked 8 minutes ago!

🇺🇿 Quyosh nuri Yerga yetib kelishi uchun ~8 daqiqa 20 soniya ketadi. Demak siz Quyoshni doim 8 daqiqa oldingi holatida ko'rasiz!

📚 So'zlar: light /laɪt/ — nur, yorug'lik; reach /riːtʃ/ — yetib bormoq

archived_posts.title: The Boy Who Cried Wolf
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Boy Who Cried Wolf

A shepherd boy often shouted "Wolf!" as a joke, and villagers ran to help — but there was no wolf. One day a real wolf came. The boy shouted, but this time nobody believed him.

💡 Moral: Liars are not believed, even when they tell the truth.
🇺🇿 Cho'pon bola hazillashib "Bo'ri!" deb baqirardi. Bir kuni haqiqiy bo'ri kelganda, hech kim ishonmadi. Xulosa: yolg'onchiga rost aytganda ham ishonishmaydi.

📚 shepherd /ˈʃepəd/ — cho'pon; villager /ˈvɪlɪdʒə/ — qishloq aholisi; believe /bɪˈliːv/ — ishonmoq

archived_posts.title: Conditional Quiz
archived_posts.text:
📝 GRAMMAR TEST

If I ____ more time, I would travel the world.

A) have
B) had
C) will have
D) having

💭 Javob 👇
archived_posts.answer:
✅ To'g'ri javob: B) had

📖 Second Conditional (hozirgi noreal holat): If + Past Simple, ... would + V1. "If I had time, I would travel."

archived_posts.title: Oxford older than Aztecs
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

Teaching at the University of Oxford began around 1096. The Aztec capital, Tenochtitlán, was founded in 1325 — so Oxford was already over two centuries old before the Aztec city existed.

🇺🇿 Oksford universitetida dars ~1096-yilda boshlangan. Atstek poytaxti Tenochtitlan esa 1325-yilda asos solingan — ya'ni Oksford undan 200 yildan oshiq oldin mavjud edi.

📚 So'zlar: founded /ˈfaʊndɪd/ — asos solingan; century /ˈsentʃəri/ — asr

archived_posts.title: Sharks older than trees
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Sharks are older than trees. Sharks have existed for about 450 million years, while the first true trees appeared around 385 million years ago.

🇺🇿 Sharklar (akula) daraxtlardan ham qadimiy. Sharklar ~450 million yildan beri mavjud, birinchi haqiqiy daraxtlar esa ~385 million yil avval paydo bo'lgan.

📚 So'zlar: appear /əˈpɪə/ — paydo bo'lmoq; million /ˈmɪljən/ — million

archived_posts.title: Phrasal Verbs
archived_posts.text:
🔤 PHRASAL VERBS

📌 give up — voz kechmoq
📌 find out — bilib olmoq
📌 carry on — davom ettirmoq

💬 Never give up — find out your weak points and carry on practising.
🇺🇿 Hech qachon voz kechmang — zaif tomonlaringizni aniqlang va mashqni davom ettiring.

archived_posts.title: The Ant and the Grasshopper
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Ant and the Grasshopper

All summer the grasshopper sang while the ant stored food for winter. When the cold came, the grasshopper was hungry and the ant was safe and warm.

💡 Moral: Prepare today for the needs of tomorrow.
🇺🇿 Yoz bo'yi chigirtka qo'shiq aytdi, chumoli esa qishga oziq yig'di. Qish kelganda chumoli to'q, chigirtka och qoldi. Xulosa: ertangi kun uchun bugundan tayyorlan.

📚 store /stɔː/ — saqlamoq, jamg'armoq; hungry /ˈhʌŋɡri/ — och; prepare /prɪˈpeə/ — tayyorlanmoq

archived_posts.title: Water expands when frozen
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Most liquids shrink when they get cold, but water expands by about 9% when it freezes. That is why ice floats on water.

🇺🇿 Ko'p suyuqliklar sovuganda qisqaradi, lekin suv muzlaganda ~9% ga kengayadi. Shu sababli muz suv ustida suzadi.

📚 So'zlar: expand /ɪkˈspænd/ — kengaymoq; freeze /friːz/ — muzlamoq; float /fləʊt/ — suzmoq

archived_posts.title: Great Wall myth
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

It is a popular myth that the Great Wall of China can be seen from space with the naked eye. In reality, it is too narrow to see without help, even from low orbit.

🇺🇿 Buyuk Xitoy devorini koinotdan oddiy ko'z bilan ko'rish mumkin degan fikr — afsona. Aslida u juda ingichka, hatto past orbitadan ham yordamchi asbobsiz ko'rinmaydi.

📚 So'zlar: myth /mɪθ/ — afsona; narrow /ˈnærəʊ/ — ingichka, tor

archived_posts.title: Describe Emotions
archived_posts.text:
🔤 VOCABULARY · Feelings

📌 anxious /ˈæŋkʃəs/ — xavotirli
📌 grateful /ˈɡreɪtfl/ — minnatdor
📌 confident /ˈkɒnfɪdənt/ — ishonchli, dadil

💬 I felt anxious before the exam, but now I am confident and grateful for my practice.
🇺🇿 Imtihondan oldin xavotirda edim, endi esa ishonchliman va mashqim uchun minnatdorman.

archived_posts.title: A day longer than a year
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

On Venus, a day is longer than a year. Venus rotates so slowly that one full rotation (about 243 Earth days) takes longer than one orbit around the Sun (about 225 Earth days).

🇺🇿 Veneradagi bir kun bir yildan uzun. Venera shunchalik sekin aylanadiki, bir marta o'q atrofida aylanishi (~243 Yer kuni) Quyosh atrofini aylanib chiqishidan (~225 Yer kuni) uzoqroq.

📚 So'zlar: rotate /rəʊˈteɪt/ — aylanmoq; orbit /ˈɔːbɪt/ — orbita

archived_posts.title: The Fox and the Grapes
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Fox and the Grapes

A hungry fox saw juicy grapes hanging high on a vine. He jumped again and again but could not reach them. At last he walked away, saying, "They are probably sour anyway."

💡 Moral: It is easy to despise what you cannot have.
🇺🇿 Och tulki baland uzumga sakraydi, lekin yetolmaydi. Ketayotib: "Baribir nordon bo'lsa kerak", deydi. Xulosa: yeta olmagan narsani kamsitish oson.

📚 vine /vaɪn/ — tok; reach /riːtʃ/ — yetmoq; sour /ˈsaʊə/ — nordon

archived_posts.title: Present Perfect
archived_posts.text:
📚 GRAMMAR · Present Perfect

have/has + V3 → o'tmishdagi ish, hozir bilan bog'liq natija.

💬 I have finished my homework. She has lived here for five years.
🇺🇿 "for" muddatni bildiradi (for 5 years), "since" boshlanish nuqtasini (since 2020).

📚 finish /ˈfɪnɪʃ/ — tugatmoq; result /rɪˈzʌlt/ — natija

archived_posts.title: Honey never spoils
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found pots of honey in ancient Egyptian tombs that are over 3,000 years old — and still edible!

🇺🇿 Asal hech qachon buzilmaydi. Arxeologlar qadimgi Misr maqbaralaridan 3000 yildan oshiq asal topishgan — u hali ham yeyishga yaroqli!

📚 So'zlar: spoil /spɔɪl/ — buzilmoq; tomb /tuːm/ — maqbara; edible /ˈedəbl/ — yeyish mumkin bo'lgan

archived_posts.title: The first programmer
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

The world's first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace, in the 1840s. She wrote an algorithm for Charles Babbage's mechanical machine, a century before modern computers existed.

🇺🇿 Dunyodagi birinchi dasturchi — Ada Lovelace (1840-yillar). U Charlz Bebbijning mexanik mashinasi uchun algoritm yozgan — zamonaviy kompyuterlardan bir asr oldin.

📚 So'zlar: algorithm /ˈælɡərɪðəm/ — algoritm; machine /məˈʃiːn/ — mashina

archived_posts.title: Essay Connectors
archived_posts.text:
🔤 IELTS WRITING CONNECTORS

📌 furthermore /ˈfɜːðəmɔː/ — bundan tashqari
📌 nevertheless /ˌnevəðəˈles/ — shunga qaramay
📌 consequently /ˈkɒnsɪkwəntli/ — natijada

💬 The plan was costly; nevertheless, it succeeded. Consequently, profits rose.
🇺🇿 Reja qimmat edi; shunga qaramay, muvaffaqiyatli bo'ldi. Natijada foyda oshdi.

archived_posts.title: The Golden Touch
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Golden Touch

King Midas wished that everything he touched would turn to gold. At first he was happy, but then his food and even his daughter turned to gold. He begged to lose the gift.

💡 Moral: Greed can destroy what we love most.
🇺🇿 Shoh Midas teккan narsasi oltinga aylanishini so'raydi. Lekin ovqati, hatto qizi ham oltinga aylanadi. Xulosa: ochko'zlik eng qadrli narsani yo'q qilishi mumkin.

📚 wish /wɪʃ/ — orzu qilmoq; greed /ɡriːd/ — ochko'zlik; destroy /dɪˈstrɔɪ/ — yo'q qilmoq

archived_posts.title: The Eiffel Tower grows
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

The Eiffel Tower can grow about 15 cm taller in summer. Heat makes the iron expand, so the metal structure rises slightly when it is hot.

🇺🇿 Eyfel minorasi yozda ~15 sm balandroq bo'lishi mumkin. Issiqlik temirni kengaytiradi, shuning uchun metall tuzilma biroz ko'tariladi.

📚 So'zlar: iron /ˈaɪən/ — temir; expand /ɪkˈspænd/ — kengaymoq; structure /ˈstrʌktʃə/ — tuzilma

archived_posts.title: Synonym Quiz
archived_posts.text:
📝 IELTS READING TEST

"The results were remarkable."
remarkable = ?

A) ordinary
B) impressive
C) boring
D) confusing

💭 Javob 👇
archived_posts.answer:
✅ To'g'ri javob: B) impressive

📖 remarkable = ajoyib, e'tiborga loyiq (impressive). Reading'da sinonimlarni bilish juda muhim.

archived_posts.title: Bukhara — Silk Road jewel
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY · Uzbekistan

Bukhara has more than 2,000 years of history and was a major centre of learning on the Silk Road. Its old city is a UNESCO World Heritage Site with hundreds of preserved monuments.

🇺🇿 Buxoro 2000 yildan ortiq tarixga ega va Ipak yo'lidagi yirik ilm markazi bo'lgan. Eski shahri YuNESKO Jahon merosi ro'yxatida, yuzlab yodgorliklari saqlangan.

📚 So'zlar: heritage /ˈherɪtɪdʒ/ — meros; preserve /prɪˈzɜːv/ — saqlamoq

archived_posts.title: Time Words
archived_posts.text:
🔤 VOCABULARY · Time

📌 rarely /ˈreəli/ — kamdan-kam
📌 frequently /ˈfriːkwəntli/ — tez-tez
📌 eventually /ɪˈventʃuəli/ — oxir-oqibat

💬 I rarely watched films in English before, but I do it frequently now, and eventually I understood them easily.
🇺🇿 Avval kamdan-kam ingliz tilida film ko'rardim, endi tez-tez ko'raman va oxir-oqibat ularni oson tushunadigan bo'ldim.

archived_posts.title: The waggle dance
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Honeybees "talk" by dancing. A bee performs a special "waggle dance" to tell other bees the direction and distance to flowers. This discovery won a Nobel Prize.

🇺🇿 Asalarilar raqs orqali "gaplashadi". Ari maxsus "waggle" raqsi bilan gullarning yo'nalishi va masofasini boshqalarga aytadi. Bu kashfiyot Nobel mukofotini olgan.

📚 So'zlar: direction /dəˈrekʃn/ — yo'nalish; distance /ˈdɪstəns/ — masofa; discovery /dɪˈskʌvəri/ — kashfiyot

archived_posts.title: The Two Travellers
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Two Travellers and the Bear

Two friends were walking when a bear appeared. One climbed a tree; the other lay still and pretended to be dead. The bear sniffed him and left. "What did it whisper?" asked the first. "It said: never trust a friend who leaves you in danger."

💡 Moral: True friends stay with you in trouble.
🇺🇿 Ayiq kelганда biri daraxtga chiqdi, ikkinchisi o'likka soxta o'xshatdi. Xulosa: haqiqiy do'st qiyinchilikda tashlab ketmaydi.

📚 pretend /prɪˈtend/ — o'zini ... qilib ko'rsatmoq; whisper /ˈwɪspə/ — pichirlamoq; trust /trʌst/ — ishonmoq

archived_posts.title: Passive Voice
archived_posts.text:
📚 GRAMMAR · Passive Voice

be + V3 → ish kimga/nimaga qilingani muhim bo'lganda.

💬 English is spoken in many countries. The bridge was built in 1990.
🇺🇿 IELTS Writing'da rasmiy uslub uchun foydali: "The data was collected from 500 people."

📚 spoken /ˈspəʊkən/ — gapiriladigan; built /bɪlt/ — qurilgan

archived_posts.title: Your brain & languages
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Learning a second language strengthens the brain. Research shows that bilingual people often have better memory and focus, and may delay age-related memory decline.

🇺🇿 Ikkinchi tilni o'rganish miyani kuchaytiradi. Tadqiqotlarga ko'ra, ikki tilli odamlarda xotira va diqqat ko'pincha kuchliroq, yoshga bog'liq xotira pasayishi kechikishi mumkin.

📚 So'zlar: bilingual /baɪˈlɪŋɡwəl/ — ikki tilli; memory /ˈmeməri/ — xotira; decline /dɪˈklaɪn/ — pasayish

archived_posts.title: Idiom: Hit the books
archived_posts.text:
🗣 IDIOM OF THE DAY

📚 "hit the books" — qattiq o'qishga kirishmoq

💬 I have an exam tomorrow, so I need to hit the books tonight.
🇺🇿 Ma'nosi "kitobni urmoq" emas — "jiddiy o'qishga kirishmoq". Imtihon oldidan ishlatiladi.

📚 exam /ɪɡˈzæm/ — imtihon; tonight /təˈnaɪt/ — bugun kechqurun

archived_posts.title: Olympic Games origin
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

The Olympic Games began in ancient Greece in 776 BC, in the town of Olympia. They were held every four years in honour of the god Zeus — a tradition that inspired the modern Olympics.

🇺🇿 Olimpiada o'yinlari qadimgi Yunonistonda, mil. av. 776-yilda Olimpiya shahrida boshlangan. Har to'rt yilda Zevs xudosi sharafiga o'tkazilgan — bu an'ana zamonaviy Olimpiadaga ilhom bergan.

📚 So'zlar: honour /ˈɒnə/ — sharaf; tradition /trəˈdɪʃn/ — an'ana; inspire /ɪnˈspaɪə/ — ilhomlantirmoq

archived_posts.title: Al-Khwarizmi & algebra
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY · Uzbekistan

Al-Khwarizmi (around 780-850), a scholar from Khwarazm, is called the father of algebra. The word 'algebra' comes from his book title, and the word 'algorithm' comes from his name.

🇺🇿 Xorazmlik olim Al-Xorazmiy (~780-850) algebra otasi hisoblanadi. 'Algebra' so'zi uning kitobi nomidan, 'algoritm' so'zi esa uning ismidan kelib chiqqan.

📚 So'zlar: scholar /ˈskɒlə/ — olim; equation /ɪˈkweɪʒn/ — tenglama

archived_posts.title: Babies have more bones
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Babies are born with about 300 bones, but adults have only 206. As we grow, some bones fuse together.

🇺🇿 Chaqaloqlar ~300 ta suyak bilan tug'iladi, lekin kattalarda atigi 206 ta bo'ladi. O'sish davomida ba'zi suyaklar birlashib ketadi.

📚 So'zlar: bone /bəʊn/ — suyak; fuse /fjuːz/ — birlashmoq; grow /ɡrəʊ/ — o'smoq

archived_posts.title: The Crow and the Pitcher
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Crow and the Pitcher

A thirsty crow found a pitcher with a little water at the bottom. Its beak could not reach it. So the clever crow dropped small stones inside, one by one, until the water rose to the top and it could drink.

💡 Moral: Little by little, cleverness solves big problems.
🇺🇿 Chanqagan qarg'a tubida ozgina suvi bor ko'zani topadi. Aql bilan toshlarni birma-bir tashlab, suvni ko'taradi va ichadi. Xulosa: aql-zakovat katta muammoni ham yechadi.

📚 thirsty /ˈθɜːsti/ — chanqagan; beak /biːk/ — tumshuq; clever /ˈklevə/ — aqlli

archived_posts.title: Antarctica is a desert
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

The largest desert on Earth is not hot — it is Antarctica. A desert is defined by very low rainfall, not by heat, and Antarctica is extremely dry.

🇺🇿 Yerdagi eng katta cho'l issiq emas — bu Antarktida. Cho'l issiqlik bilan emas, juda kam yog'in bilan belgilanadi, Antarktida esa o'ta quruq.

📚 So'zlar: desert /ˈdezət/ — cho'l; rainfall /ˈreɪnfɔːl/ — yog'in; dry /draɪ/ — quruq

archived_posts.title: Describe Change
archived_posts.text:
🔤 IELTS VOCABULARY · Change

📌 rise /raɪz/ — ko'tarilmoq
📌 drop /drɒp/ — tushmoq
📌 remain stable — barqaror qolmoq

💬 Sales rose sharply, then dropped, and finally remained stable.
🇺🇿 Sotuvlar keskin ko'tarildi, keyin tushdi va nihoyat barqaror qoldi.

archived_posts.title: Ibn Sina (Avicenna)
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY · Uzbekistan

Ibn Sina (Avicenna, 980-1037), born near Bukhara, was one of history's greatest thinkers. His book 'The Canon of Medicine' was used in European universities for over 600 years.

🇺🇿 Buxoro yaqinida tug'ilgan Ibn Sino (980-1037) tarixning buyuk allomalaridan biri. Uning 'Tib qonunlari' asari Yevropa universitetlarida 600 yildan ortiq o'qitilgan.

📚 So'zlar: medicine /ˈmedsn/ — tibbiyot; thinker /ˈθɪŋkə/ — mutafakkir

archived_posts.title: Idiom: Break a leg
archived_posts.text:
🗣 IDIOM OF THE DAY

🍀 'break a leg' — omad tilayman (sahna/imtihon oldidan)

💬 You have a speaking test today? Break a leg!
🇺🇿 So'zma-so'z 'oyog'ingni sindir' emas — ma'nosi 'omad tilayman'. Aktyorlar va talabalarga aytiladi.

📚 luck /lʌk/ — omad; stage /steɪdʒ/ — sahna

archived_posts.title: No sound in space
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

There is no sound in space. Sound needs a medium like air or water to travel, but space is a vacuum — almost empty.

🇺🇿 Koinotda ovoz yo'q. Ovoz tarqalishi uchun havo yoki suv kabi muhit kerak, koinot esa vakuum — deyarli bo'sh.

📚 So'zlar: sound /saʊnd/ — ovoz; vacuum /ˈvækjuːm/ — vakuum; empty /ˈempti/ — bo'sh

archived_posts.title: The North Wind and the Sun
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The North Wind and the Sun

The wind and the sun argued who was stronger. They tried to make a traveller remove his coat. The wind blew hard, but the man held his coat tighter. Then the sun shone warmly, and the man took it off himself.

💡 Moral: Kindness is stronger than force.
🇺🇿 Shamol va quyosh kim kuchli deb bahslashadi. Shamol qancha ursa, yo'lovchi paltosini mahkam ushlaydi; quyosh iliq nur sochganda, u o'zi yechadi. Xulosa: mehribonlik kuchdan kuchli.

📚 argue /ˈɑːɡjuː/ — bahslashmoq; blow /bləʊ/ — esmoq; force /fɔːs/ — kuch, zo'rlik

archived_posts.title: Saturn could float
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Saturn is so light for its size that it would float in water. Its average density is lower than water — it is mostly gas.

🇺🇿 Saturn hajmiga nisbatan shunchalik yengilki, u suvda suzib qolardi. Uning o'rtacha zichligi suvdan past — u asosan gazdan iborat.

📚 So'zlar: float /fləʊt/ — suzmoq; density /ˈdensəti/ — zichlik; gas /ɡæs/ — gaz

archived_posts.title: Ulugh Beg the astronomer
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY · Uzbekistan

Ulugh Beg (1394-1449), ruler of Samarkand, was also a great astronomer. He built a huge observatory and measured the stars so accurately that his star catalogue was used for centuries.

🇺🇿 Samarqand hukmdori Ulug'bek (1394-1449) buyuk astronom ham edi. U ulkan rasadxona qurib, yulduzlarni shunchalik aniq o'lchaganki, uning yulduzlar jadvalidan asrlar davomida foydalanilgan.

📚 So'zlar: astronomer /əˈstrɒnəmə/ — astronom; observatory /əbˈzɜːvətri/ — rasadxona; accurate /ˈækjərət/ — aniq

archived_posts.title: Opinion Phrases
archived_posts.text:
🔤 SPEAKING PHRASES · Opinion

📌 In my opinion — mening fikrimcha
📌 I believe that — menimcha
📌 It seems to me — menga shunday tuyuladi

💬 In my opinion, reading every day is the best way to learn.
🇺🇿 IELTS Speaking'da fikr bildirishda bu iboralar tabiiy eshitiladi.

archived_posts.title: The fastest land animal
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

The cheetah is the fastest land animal. It can run up to about 110 km/h in short bursts to catch its prey.

🇺🇿 Gepard quruqlikdagi eng tez hayvon. U o'ljasini tutish uchun qisqa masofada soatiga ~110 km tezlikda yugura oladi.

📚 So'zlar: fast /fɑːst/ — tez; prey /preɪ/ — o'lja; catch /kætʃ/ — tutmoq

archived_posts.title: a / an / the
archived_posts.text:
📚 GRAMMAR · Articles

'a/an' — noaniq (birinchi marta): a book, an apple.
'the' — aniq (ma'lum narsa): the book on my desk.

💬 I saw a dog. The dog was friendly.
🇺🇿 'a' undosh tovushdan oldin, 'an' unli tovushdan oldin: a car, an hour.

📚 noun /naʊn/ — ot; vowel /ˈvaʊəl/ — unli

archived_posts.title: The Goose and Golden Eggs
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Goose with Golden Eggs

A farmer had a goose that laid one golden egg every day. Greedy for more, he cut the goose open to get all the gold at once — but found nothing, and lost the goose.

💡 Moral: Greed often destroys the source of good fortune.
🇺🇿 Dehqonning g'ozi har kuni bitta oltin tuxum qo'yardi. Ochko'zlik qilib, hammasini birdan olmoqchi bo'lib g'ozni so'yadi va hammasidan ayriladi. Xulosa: ochko'zlik baxt manbasini yo'q qiladi.

📚 goose /ɡuːs/ — g'oz; greedy /ˈɡriːdi/ — ochko'z; fortune /ˈfɔːtʃuːn/ — baxt, boylik

archived_posts.title: The Moon drifts away
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

The Moon is slowly moving away from Earth — about 3.8 cm every year. That is roughly the speed at which your fingernails grow.

🇺🇿 Oy Yerdan sekin uzoqlashmoqda — har yili ~3.8 sm. Bu taxminan tirnoq o'sish tezligiga teng.

📚 So'zlar: move away — uzoqlashmoq; roughly /ˈrʌfli/ — taxminan; grow /ɡrəʊ/ — o'smoq

archived_posts.title: Paper from China
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

Paper was invented in China around 105 AD by Cai Lun. Before paper, people wrote on silk, bamboo, or clay — much heavier and more expensive.

🇺🇿 Qog'oz Xitoyda ~105-yilda Tsay Lun tomonidan ixtiro qilingan. Undan oldin odamlar ipak, bambuk yoki loyga yozgan — ancha og'ir va qimmat.

📚 So'zlar: invent /ɪnˈvent/ — ixtiro qilmoq; expensive /ɪkˈspensɪv/ — qimmat

archived_posts.title: Academic Verbs
archived_posts.text:
🔤 ACADEMIC VERBS · C1

📌 demonstrate /ˈdemənstreɪt/ — namoyish etmoq, ko'rsatmoq
📌 indicate /ˈɪndɪkeɪt/ — bildirmoq, ko'rsatib turmoq
📌 enhance /ɪnˈhɑːns/ — kuchaytirmoq, yaxshilamoq

💬 The study demonstrates that reading enhances vocabulary and indicates clear progress.
🇺🇿 Tadqiqot o'qish so'z boyligini kuchaytirishini ko'rsatadi va aniq o'sishni bildiradi.

archived_posts.title: Bats can truly fly
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Bats are the only mammals that can truly fly. Other 'flying' animals, like flying squirrels, only glide through the air.

🇺🇿 Ko'rshapalaklar haqiqatan ucha oladigan yagona sutemizuvchilardir. Boshqa 'uchuvchi' hayvonlar, masalan uchuvchi olmaxonlar, faqat havoda sirpanadi.

📚 So'zlar: mammal /ˈmæml/ — sutemizuvchi; fly /flaɪ/ — uchmoq; glide /ɡlaɪd/ — sirpanmoq

archived_posts.title: The Dog and the Bone
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Dog and Its Reflection

A dog carried a bone across a bridge. In the water he saw his own reflection and thought it was another dog with a bigger bone. He barked to grab it — and dropped his own bone into the river.

💡 Moral: Be content; greed can cost you what you already have.
🇺🇿 It suyakni ko'tarib ko'prikdan o'tar edi. Suvda o'z aksini ko'rib, boshqa it deb o'yladi va tortib olmoqchi bo'lib o'z suyagini suvga tushirib yubordi. Xulosa: ochko'zlik boringni ham yo'qotadi.

📚 bone /bəʊn/ — suyak; reflection /rɪˈflekʃn/ — aks; content /kənˈtent/ — qanoatli

archived_posts.title: Bananas share our DNA
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Humans share about 60% of their DNA with bananas. All living things use the same basic genetic code, so we have many genes in common with plants.

🇺🇿 Insonlar DNK'sining ~60% bananlar bilan umumiy. Barcha tirik mavjudotlar bir xil genetik kodga ega, shuning uchun bizda o'simliklar bilan ham ko'p umumiy genlar bor.

📚 So'zlar: gene /dʒiːn/ — gen; common /ˈkɒmən/ — umumiy; living /ˈlɪvɪŋ/ — tirik

archived_posts.title: First powered flight
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

The Wright brothers made the first powered flight in 1903. It lasted only 12 seconds and covered about 37 metres — shorter than the wingspan of a modern jet.

🇺🇿 Rayt aka-ukalari 1903-yilda birinchi motorli parvozni amalga oshirgan. U atigi 12 soniya davom etib, ~37 metr uchgan — bu zamonaviy samolyot qanotidan ham qisqa.

📚 So'zlar: flight /flaɪt/ — parvoz; last /lɑːst/ — davom etmoq; cover /ˈkʌvə/ — bosib o'tmoq

archived_posts.title: Idiom: Under the weather
archived_posts.text:
🗣 IDIOM OF THE DAY

🤒 'under the weather' — o'zini yomon his qilmoq, betobroq

💬 I can't come today; I'm feeling under the weather.
🇺🇿 So'zma-so'z 'ob-havo ostida' emas — ma'nosi 'kasalroq, mazasi yo'q'.

📚 ill /ɪl/ — kasal; tired /ˈtaɪəd/ — charchagan

archived_posts.title: Linking Words
archived_posts.text:
🔤 LINKING WORDS

📌 however /haʊˈevə/ — biroq
📌 because /bɪˈkɒz/ — chunki
📌 therefore /ˈðeəfɔː/ — shuning uchun

💬 I studied hard; therefore, I passed. The test was hard, however, I stayed calm.
🇺🇿 Bu bog'lovchilar gaplarni mantiqiy bog'laydi va yozma ishni yaxshilaydi.

archived_posts.title: Most of Earth is water
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by water, but over 96% of all that water is salty ocean. Only a tiny part is fresh water we can drink.

🇺🇿 Yer yuzasining ~71% suv bilan qoplangan, lekin bu suvning 96% dan ortig'i sho'r okean. Faqat juda oz qismi ichish mumkin bo'lgan chuchuk suv.

📚 So'zlar: surface /ˈsɜːfɪs/ — yuza; salty /ˈsɔːlti/ — sho'r; fresh /freʃ/ — chuchuk

archived_posts.title: The Bundle of Sticks
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Bundle of Sticks

An old man gave each son a single stick to break — easy. Then he tied many sticks together and asked them to break the bundle. No one could. 'Together,' he said, 'you cannot be broken.'

💡 Moral: Unity is strength.
🇺🇿 Chol o'g'illariga bittadan tayoq beradi — oson sinadi. Keyin bog'lamni berganda, hech kim sindirolmaydi. 'Birlashsangiz, sizni hech kim sindirolmaydi', deydi. Xulosa: birlik — kuch.

📚 stick /stɪk/ — tayoq; bundle /ˈbʌndl/ — bog'lam; unity /ˈjuːnəti/ — birlik

archived_posts.title: Penguins live in the south
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Wild penguins live almost only in the Southern Hemisphere. You will not find wild penguins at the North Pole — polar bears live there instead.

🇺🇿 Yovvoyi pingvinlar deyarli faqat Janubiy yarimsharda yashaydi. Shimoliy qutbda yovvoyi pingvin yo'q — u yerda oq ayiqlar yashaydi.

📚 So'zlar: wild /waɪld/ — yovvoyi; pole /pəʊl/ — qutb; instead /ɪnˈsted/ — o'rniga

archived_posts.title: First human in space
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

Yuri Gagarin became the first human in space on 12 April 1961. His flight around the Earth lasted just 108 minutes.

🇺🇿 Yuriy Gagarin 1961-yil 12-aprelda koinotga uchgan birinchi inson bo'ldi. Uning Yer atrofidagi parvozi atigi 108 daqiqa davom etgan.

📚 So'zlar: space /speɪs/ — koinot; flight /flaɪt/ — parvoz; orbit /ˈɔːbɪt/ — orbita

archived_posts.title: Strong Adjectives
archived_posts.text:
🔤 STRONG ADJECTIVES

📌 exhausted /ɪɡˈzɔːstɪd/ — juda charchagan (very tired)
📌 delighted /dɪˈlaɪtɪd/ — juda xursand (very happy)
📌 terrified /ˈterɪfaɪd/ — juda qo'rqqan (very scared)

💬 After the exam I was exhausted, but delighted with my score.
🇺🇿 Kuchli sifatlar 'very + oddiy sifat' o'rniga ishlatiladi va nutqni boyitadi.

archived_posts.title: Diamonds are carbon
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Diamonds and pencil lead are both made of pure carbon. The only difference is how the atoms are arranged — that is why one is the hardest natural material and the other is soft.

🇺🇿 Olmos ham, qalam grafiti ham sof uglerod (carbon). Farqi faqat atomlarning joylashuvida — shuning uchun biri eng qattiq, ikkinchisi yumshoq.

📚 So'zlar: carbon /ˈkɑːbən/ — uglerod; atom /ˈætəm/ — atom; arrange /əˈreɪndʒ/ — joylashtirmoq

archived_posts.title: The Ant and the Dove
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Ant and the Dove

A dove saw an ant drowning in a river and dropped a leaf for it to climb on. Later, a hunter aimed at the dove. The grateful ant bit his foot, the hunter missed, and the dove flew away safely.

💡 Moral: One good turn deserves another.
🇺🇿 Kaptar cho'kayotgan chumoliga barg tashlab qutqaradi. Keyin ovchi kaptarga mo'ljal olganda, chumoli uni chaqib, kaptar qutuladi. Xulosa: yaxshilik yaxshilik bilan qaytadi.

📚 drown /draʊn/ — cho'kmoq; grateful /ˈɡreɪtfl/ — minnatdor; aim /eɪm/ — mo'ljal olmoq

archived_posts.title: Your heart, every day
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Your heart beats around 100,000 times every day, pumping blood through about 100,000 km of blood vessels — enough to circle the Earth more than twice.

🇺🇿 Yuragingiz har kuni ~100,000 marta uradi va qonni ~100,000 km qon tomirlari orqali haydaydi — bu Yerni ikki martadan ko'proq o'rab oladi.

📚 So'zlar: beat /biːt/ — urmoq; blood vessel /ˈvesl/ — qon tomir; circle /ˈsɜːkl/ — o'rab olmoq

archived_posts.title: Inventions from China
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

Four great inventions came from ancient China: paper, printing, gunpowder, and the compass. They changed the whole world.

🇺🇿 Qadimgi Xitoydan to'rtta buyuk ixtiro chiqqan: qog'oz, bosmaxona, porox va kompas. Ular butun dunyoni o'zgartirgan.

📚 So'zlar: invention /ɪnˈvenʃn/ — ixtiro; printing /ˈprɪntɪŋ/ — bosmaxona; compass /ˈkʌmpəs/ — kompas

archived_posts.title: Idiom: Bite the bullet
archived_posts.text:
🗣 IDIOM OF THE DAY

😬 'bite the bullet' — qiyin ishga jur'at qilib kirishmoq, chidamoq

💬 I was afraid of speaking English, but I bit the bullet and joined the club.
🇺🇿 Ma'nosi 'noxush, lekin zarur ishni qat'iyat bilan bajarmoq'.

📚 brave /breɪv/ — jasur; decision /dɪˈsɪʒn/ — qaror

archived_posts.title: Hottest planet
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Venus is the hottest planet, not Mercury — even though Mercury is closer to the Sun. Venus has a thick atmosphere that traps heat, reaching about 465°C.

🇺🇿 Eng issiq sayyora Merkuriy emas, Venera — garchi Merkuriy Quyoshga yaqinroq bo'lsa-da. Venerada issiqlikni ushlab qoluvchi qalin atmosfera bor, harorat ~465°C ga yetadi.

📚 So'zlar: planet /ˈplænɪt/ — sayyora; atmosphere /ˈætməsfɪə/ — atmosfera; trap /træp/ — ushlab qolmoq

archived_posts.title: Travel Words
archived_posts.text:
🔤 VOCABULARY · Travel

📌 journey /ˈdʒɜːni/ — sayohat, yo'l
📌 luggage /ˈlʌɡɪdʒ/ — yuk, chamadon
📌 abroad /əˈbrɔːd/ — chet elda

💬 My journey abroad was long, but I packed my luggage well.
🇺🇿 'travel' umumiy so'z, 'journey' bitta yo'l/sayohat, 'trip' qisqa borib-kelish.

archived_posts.title: Town Mouse, Country Mouse
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Town Mouse and the Country Mouse

A town mouse visited his country cousin and laughed at the simple food. He invited him to the city's feasts — but there, cats and dangers never stopped. The country mouse went home, preferring peace to luxury.

💡 Moral: A simple, safe life is better than a rich, fearful one.
🇺🇿 Shahar sichqoni qishloqdagi jo'rasini oddiy ovqat uchun mazax qiladi. Lekin shaharda mushuk va xavf to'xtamaydi. Qishloq sichqoni tinchlikni afzal ko'rib uyiga qaytadi.

📚 feast /fiːst/ — ziyofat; danger /ˈdeɪndʒə/ — xavf; peace /piːs/ — tinchlik

archived_posts.title: Snails and sleep
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Some snails can sleep for up to three years when the weather is too dry or cold, slowing their bodies down to survive.

🇺🇿 Ba'zi shilliqqurtlar ob-havo juda quruq yoki sovuq bo'lganda uch yilgacha uxlay oladi — tanasini sekinlashtirib, omon qoladi.

📚 So'zlar: snail /sneɪl/ — shilliqqurt; survive /səˈvaɪv/ — omon qolmoq; weather /ˈweðə/ — ob-havo

archived_posts.title: Comparatives
archived_posts.text:
📚 GRAMMAR · Comparatives

Qisqa sifat + -er: big → bigger. Uzun sifat: more + sifat: more useful.

💬 English is easier than I thought, but more useful than any other skill.
🇺🇿 'than' taqqoslashda ishlatiladi. Istisno: good → better, bad → worse.

📚 compare /kəmˈpeə/ — taqqoslamoq; easy /ˈiːzi/ — oson

archived_posts.title: Registan of Samarkand
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY · Uzbekistan

The Registan in Samarkand was the heart of the medieval city — a public square framed by three grand madrasahs (Islamic schools). Today it is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a symbol of Uzbekistan.

🇺🇿 Samarqanddagi Registon o'rta asr shaharning markazi bo'lgan — uchta ulug'vor madrasa bilan o'ralgan maydon. Bugun u YuNESKO merosi va O'zbekiston ramzi.

📚 So'zlar: square /skweə/ — maydon; symbol /ˈsɪmbl/ — ramz; heritage /ˈherɪtɪdʒ/ — meros

archived_posts.title: Common Collocations
archived_posts.text:
🔤 COLLOCATIONS

📌 make a decision — qaror qabul qilmoq
📌 take a break — tanaffus qilmoq
📌 pay attention — e'tibor bermoq

💬 Pay attention in class, take a short break, then make a decision calmly.
🇺🇿 Inglizchada ba'zi fe'l+ot juftliklari doim birga ishlatiladi (collocations) — ularni birga yodlang.

archived_posts.title: Your brain at night
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Your brain is very active while you sleep. During sleep it organises memories and information from the day — which is why good sleep helps you learn and remember.

🇺🇿 Miya uxlayotganda ham juda faol. U kun davomidagi xotira va ma'lumotlarni tartibga soladi — shuning uchun yaxshi uyqu o'rganish va eslab qolishga yordam beradi.

📚 So'zlar: active /ˈæktɪv/ — faol; organise /ˈɔːɡənaɪz/ — tartiblamoq; remember /rɪˈmembə/ — eslamoq

archived_posts.title: Idiom: Spill the beans
archived_posts.text:
🗣 IDIOM OF THE DAY

🫘 'spill the beans' — sirni oshkor qilmoq

💬 Come on, spill the beans — what happened at the meeting?
🇺🇿 So'zma-so'z 'loviyani to'kmoq' emas — ma'nosi 'sirni aytib qo'ymoq'.

📚 secret /ˈsiːkrət/ — sir; reveal /rɪˈviːl/ — oshkor qilmoq

archived_posts.title: Belling the Cat
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · Belling the Cat

The mice wanted to know when the cat was coming, so they decided to tie a bell around its neck. It was a great plan — until one old mouse asked, 'But who will put the bell on the cat?'

💡 Moral: It is easy to suggest a plan; doing it is the hard part.
🇺🇿 Sichqonlar mushuk kelishini bilish uchun bo'yniga qo'ng'iroq bog'lashga qaror qiladi. Ammo bir keksa sichqon so'raydi: 'Kim bog'laydi?' Xulosa: reja taklif qilish oson, bajarish qiyin.

📚 bell /bel/ — qo'ng'iroq; suggest /səˈdʒest/ — taklif qilmoq; plan /plæn/ — reja

archived_posts.title: Why the sky is blue
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

The sky is blue because air scatters blue light from the Sun more than other colours. At sunset the light travels farther, so we see red and orange instead.

🇺🇿 Osmon ko'k, chunki havo Quyoshdan kelgan ko'k nurni boshqa ranglardan ko'proq sochadi. Kun botishda nur uzoqroq yo'l bosadi, shuning uchun qizil va to'q sariqni ko'ramiz.

📚 So'zlar: scatter /ˈskætə/ — sochmoq; sunset /ˈsʌnset/ — kun botishi; colour /ˈkʌlə/ — rang

archived_posts.title: Edison and the light bulb
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

Thomas Edison did not invent the first light bulb, but in 1879 he created one that was practical and long-lasting. He famously said genius is '1% inspiration and 99% perspiration'.

🇺🇿 Tomas Edison birinchi lampochkani ixtiro qilmagan, lekin 1879-yilda amaliy va uzoq ishlaydiganini yaratgan. Uning mashhur so'zi: daholik '1% ilhom va 99% mehnat'.

📚 So'zlar: practical /ˈpræktɪkl/ — amaliy; genius /ˈdʒiːniəs/ — daho; effort /ˈefət/ — mehnat

archived_posts.title: Food & Cooking
archived_posts.text:
🔤 VOCABULARY · Food

📌 delicious /dɪˈlɪʃəs/ — mazali
📌 recipe /ˈresəpi/ — retsept
📌 ingredient /ɪnˈɡriːdiənt/ — masalliq

💬 This recipe needs only five ingredients, and the result is delicious.
🇺🇿 'recipe' (retsept) va 'receipt' /rɪˈsiːt/ (chek) — yozilishi o'xshash, ma'nosi boshqa!

archived_posts.title: Elephants and memory
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Elephants have excellent memories. They can remember other elephants and places for many years, and even recognise old friends after a long time apart.

🇺🇿 Fillarning xotirasi juda kuchli. Ular boshqa fillar va joylarni yillab eslab qoladi, hatto uzoq ajralishdan keyin ham eski do'stlarini taniydi.

📚 So'zlar: memory /ˈmeməri/ — xotira; remember /rɪˈmembə/ — eslamoq; recognise /ˈrekəɡnaɪz/ — tanimoq

archived_posts.title: The Wolf in Sheep's Clothing
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Wolf in Sheep's Clothing

A wolf put on a sheepskin and slipped in among the flock to hunt easily. But that evening the shepherd, wanting meat, picked the disguised wolf by mistake — and so the trick destroyed the trickster.

💡 Moral: Deception often traps the deceiver.
🇺🇿 Bo'ri qo'y terisini kiyib, podaga qo'shiladi. Lekin kechqurun cho'pon go'sht uchun aynan o'sha 'qo'y'ni tanlaydi. Xulosa: hiyla ko'pincha hiylagarning o'zini tutadi.

📚 disguise /dɪsˈɡaɪz/ — niqoblamoq; flock /flɒk/ — poda; deceive /dɪˈsiːv/ — aldamoq

archived_posts.title: Tomato is a fruit
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Botanically, a tomato is a fruit, not a vegetable, because it grows from a flower and contains seeds. In cooking, though, we usually treat it as a vegetable.

🇺🇿 Botanika bo'yicha pomidor — meva, sabzavot emas, chunki u guldan o'sadi va urug'i bor. Lekin oshxonada uni sabzavot sifatida ishlatamiz.

📚 So'zlar: fruit /fruːt/ — meva; seed /siːd/ — urug'; vegetable /ˈvedʒtəbl/ — sabzavot

archived_posts.title: The Silk Road
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY FACT

The Silk Road was a network of trade routes linking China to Europe for over 1,500 years. Cities of Uzbekistan, like Samarkand and Bukhara, were vital stops where goods and ideas were exchanged.

🇺🇿 Ipak yo'li 1500 yildan ortiq Xitoyni Yevropa bilan bog'lagan savdo yo'llari tarmog'i edi. Samarqand va Buxoro kabi O'zbekiston shaharlari mol va g'oyalar almashinadigan muhim bekatlar bo'lgan.

📚 So'zlar: trade /treɪd/ — savdo; route /ruːt/ — yo'l; exchange /ɪksˈtʃeɪndʒ/ — almashmoq

archived_posts.title: Express Certainty
archived_posts.text:
🔤 CERTAINTY · C1

📌 undoubtedly /ʌnˈdaʊtɪdli/ — shubhasiz
📌 presumably /prɪˈzjuːməbli/ — ehtimol
📌 arguably /ˈɑːɡjuəbli/ — bahsli ravishda (eng...)

💬 Reading is arguably the best way to learn, and it undoubtedly builds vocabulary.
🇺🇿 Bu so'zlar fikrga ishonch yoki ehtiyotkorlik darajasini qo'shadi — IELTS 7.0+ uchun foydali.

archived_posts.title: Hot water tricks
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Ice floats because frozen water is about 9% less dense than liquid water. This is rare — most substances are denser when solid, but water is special, and that is why lakes freeze from the top down.

🇺🇿 Muz suzadi, chunki muzlagan suv suyuq suvdan ~9% kam zich. Bu noyob hodisa — ko'p moddalar qattiq holatda zichroq, lekin suv alohida, shuning uchun ko'llar yuzasidan muzlaydi.

📚 So'zlar: dense /dens/ — zich; solid /ˈsɒlɪd/ — qattiq; rare /reə/ — noyob

archived_posts.title: Idiom: Cost an arm and a leg
archived_posts.text:
🗣 IDIOM OF THE DAY

💸 'cost an arm and a leg' — juda qimmat turmoq

💬 That new phone costs an arm and a leg!
🇺🇿 So'zma-so'z emas — ma'nosi 'juda qimmat'. Suhbatda narx haqida ishlatiladi.

📚 expensive /ɪkˈspensɪv/ — qimmat; price /praɪs/ — narx

archived_posts.title: The Milkmaid's Dream
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Milkmaid and Her Pail

A girl carried milk on her head and dreamed of selling it, buying eggs, then chickens, then a fine dress. Lost in her daydream, she tossed her head proudly — and spilled all the milk.

💡 Moral: Do not count your chickens before they hatch.
🇺🇿 Qiz sut ko'tarib, uni sotib boyish haqida xayol suradi. Xayolga berilib boshini silkitadi va sutni to'kib yuboradi. Xulosa: ish bitmasdan natijani sanama.

📚 pail /peɪl/ — chelak; dream /driːm/ — orzu; spill /spɪl/ — to'kmoq

archived_posts.title: Blood is always red
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Human blood is always red, never blue. Veins look blue through the skin only because of how light passes through your body — but the blood inside is dark red.

🇺🇿 Inson qoni doim qizil, hech qachon ko'k emas. Tomirlar teri ostida ko'k ko'rinadi, chunki yorug'lik tana orqali shunday o'tadi — lekin ichidagi qon to'q qizil.

📚 So'zlar: blood /blʌd/ — qon; vein /veɪn/ — tomir; skin /skɪn/ — teri

archived_posts.title: Al-Beruni the scholar
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY · Uzbekistan

Al-Beruni (973-1048), born in Khwarazm, was a brilliant scholar who studied astronomy, geography and history. He estimated the Earth's radius with remarkable accuracy a thousand years ago.

🇺🇿 Xorazmda tug'ilgan Abu Rayhon Beruniy (973-1048) astronomiya, geografiya va tarixni o'rgangan buyuk olim. U ming yil avval Yer radiusini hayratlanarli aniqlikda hisoblagan.

📚 So'zlar: scholar /ˈskɒlə/ — olim; estimate /ˈestɪmeɪt/ — taxminan hisoblamoq; accuracy /ˈækjərəsi/ — aniqlik

archived_posts.title: will vs going to
archived_posts.text:
📚 GRAMMAR · Future

'will' — shu zahoti qaror yoki bashorat: I will help you.
'going to' — reja yoki ko'rinib turgan natija: I'm going to study tonight.

💬 Look at those clouds — it's going to rain. OK, I'll take an umbrella.
🇺🇿 Reja oldindan bor bo'lsa 'going to', shu onda qaror qilsangiz 'will'.

📚 plan /plæn/ — reja; decision /dɪˈsɪʒn/ — qaror

archived_posts.title: Bees and flowers
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Bees help grow much of our food. As they move from flower to flower for nectar, they carry pollen, which lets many plants and fruits grow.

🇺🇿 Asalarilar oziq-ovqatimizning ko'p qismini yetishtirishga yordam beradi. Guldan gulga nektar uchun uchganda, ular changni tashiydi va ko'p o'simlik va mevalar o'sadi.

📚 So'zlar: bee /biː/ — asalari; pollen /ˈpɒlən/ — chang; grow /ɡrəʊ/ — o'smoq

archived_posts.title: Health Words
archived_posts.text:
🔤 VOCABULARY · Health

📌 healthy /ˈhelθi/ — sog'lom
📌 exercise /ˈeksəsaɪz/ — mashq, sport
📌 sleep well — yaxshi uxlamoq

💬 To stay healthy, exercise often, eat well, and sleep well.
🇺🇿 'healthy' — sog'lom (sifat), 'health' /helθ/ — sog'liq (ot).

archived_posts.title: Bibi-Khanym Mosque
archived_posts.text:
🏛 HISTORY · Uzbekistan

The Bibi-Khanym Mosque in Samarkand, built in the early 1400s under Amir Temur, was one of the largest mosques of the Islamic world at the time. Its huge entrance still amazes visitors today.

🇺🇿 Samarqanddagi Bibixonim masjidi 1400-yillar boshida Amir Temur davrida qurilgan va o'sha davrda islom olamining eng yirik masjidlaridan biri bo'lgan. Uning ulkan darvozasi bugun ham hayratga soladi.

📚 So'zlar: mosque /mɒsk/ — masjid; entrance /ˈentrəns/ — darvoza, kirish; amaze /əˈmeɪz/ — hayratlantirmoq

archived_posts.title: The Oak and the Reeds
archived_posts.text:
📖 SHORT STORY · The Oak and the Reeds

A proud oak tree mocked the thin reeds that bent in every wind. Then a great storm came. The oak stood stiff and was torn from the ground, while the reeds bent low and survived.

💡 Moral: It is better to bend than to break.
🇺🇿 Mag'rur eman shamolda egilgan qamishlarni mazax qiladi. Bo'ron kelganda eman qattiq turib sinadi, qamishlar esa egilib omon qoladi. Xulosa: sinishdan ko'ra egilish yaxshiroq.

📚 proud /praʊd/ — mag'rur; bend /bend/ — egilmoq; storm /stɔːm/ — bo'ron

archived_posts.title: More bacteria than cells
archived_posts.text:
🔬 SCIENCE FACT

Your body contains trillions of bacteria — roughly as many bacterial cells as human cells. Most of them are helpful, especially in your gut, where they aid digestion.

🇺🇿 Tanangizda trillionlab bakteriya bor — taxminan inson hujayralari qadar. Ularning ko'pi foydali, ayniqsa ichakda ovqat hazmiga yordam beradi.

📚 So'zlar: bacteria /bækˈtɪəriə/ — bakteriya; cell /sel/ — hujayra; digestion /daɪˈdʒestʃən/ — hazm

archived_posts.title: Word Form Quiz
archived_posts.text:
📝 VOCABULARY TEST

Choose the correct word:

Her English improved ____ after daily practice.

A) significant
B) significantly
C) significance
D) signify

💭 Javob 👇
archived_posts.answer:
✅ To'g'ri javob: B) significantly

📖 Fe'lni (improved) izohlash uchun ravish (adverb) kerak: significantly. A — sifat, C — ot, D — fe'l.

archived_posts.title: Never Give Up
archived_posts.text:
🔤 MOTIVATION WORDS

📌 persist /pəˈsɪst/ — qat'iyat bilan davom etmoq
📌 progress /ˈprəʊɡres/ — taraqqiyot
📌 succeed /səkˈsiːd/ — muvaffaqiyatga erishmoq

💬 If you persist a little every day, you will see progress and finally succeed.
🇺🇿 Har kuni ozgina qat'iyat bilan ishlasangiz, taraqqiyotni ko'rasiz va muvaffaqiyatga erishasiz.
